## Extended 16-Day Forecast (Open-Meteo) — Through July 2, 2026

### Cizre — The Hottest Location
Cizre is the hottest of the four cities, consistently forecast at **39–42°C** through the end of June:
- **Jun 18**: 40.8°C / 26.5°C
- **Jun 19**: **41.9°C** / 24.4°C ← near-term peak
- **Jun 20**: 40.9°C / 25.8°C
- **Jun 21**: 38.2°C / 21.7°C (brief cooldown)
- **Jun 22–28**: 39.7–41.0°C daily highs
- **Jul 1**: 41.4°C
- **Jul 2**: **44.3°C** ← major heat surge forecast

### Şanlıurfa
- **Jun 18**: 37.6°C
- **Jun 19–20**: Slight dip to 33–35°C
- **Jun 25–26**: Rebound to **38.4–39.9°C**
- **Jun 30**: 39.4°C
- **Jul 2**: **40.7°C**

### Diyarbakır
- **Jun 18**: 37.3°C
- **Jun 19**: 37.6°C
- **Jun 26–28**: **39.1–40.4°C** — approaching heat wave threshold
- **Jul 2**: **41.0°C**

### Şırnak (city)
- Consistently cooler due to higher elevation (~1,400 m)
- Range: 30.5–35.8°C through the forecast period
- **Jul 2**: 35.8°C peak

## Emerging Heat Wave Pattern (Late June / Early July)

The forecast data shows a **building heat pattern** with temperatures escalating toward the end of June and into early July:
- **Cizre** is forecast to reach **44.3°C on July 2**, which would be an extraordinary reading for any date
- **Diyarbakır** and **Şanlıurfa** both projected to reach **40–41°C** in the same timeframe
- Dust events are forecast for June 20 across the region, consistent with hot southerly air advection

## Historical Context (June Climatology)

### June 2024 (observed)
| City | Avg Max | Avg Min | Peak Max |
|------|---------|---------|----------|
| Şanlıurfa | 38.7°C | 25.2°C | **43.2°C** (Jun 20) |
| Diyarbakır | 37.5°C | 21.3°C | **41.3°C** (Jun 20) |
| Cizre | 40.0°C | 23.7°C | **43.3°C** (Jun 20) |

### June 2025 (observed)
| City | Avg Max | Avg Min | Peak Max |
|------|---------|---------|----------|
| Şanlıurfa | 36.3°C | 22.7°C | **41.3°C** (Jun 28) |
| Diyarbakır | 35.6°C | 21.0°C | **40.8°C** |
| Cizre | 39.5°C | 23.0°C | **44.8°C** (Jun 28) |

### Current 2026 vs. Historical Comparison
- Current forecast highs (37–41°C) are **within normal range** for mid-June in southeastern Turkey
- The region routinely sees 40°C+ in June, especially at low-elevation locations like Cizre
- Cizre's June 2025 peak of **44.8°C** shows these cities can reach extreme heat levels
- The Jul 2 forecast of **44.3°C** for Cizre, if realized, would match the high end of recent June/July observations

## Key Assessment

- **No anomalous heat wave is currently underway** as of June 18, 2026 — temperatures are within the normal-to-slightly-below-average range for mid-June
- **A building heat pattern** is forecast for late June, with temperatures ramping up toward 40°C+ across the region by June 25–30
- **Cizre is the critical hotspot**, consistently the hottest location, with forecast highs of 41–44°C
- **Dust/sandstorm events** are forecast around June 20, which often accompany heat surges
- **The most significant heat** is projected for **late June through early July 2**, when multiple cities may exceed 40°C simultaneously
- Southeastern Turkey's semi-arid, low-elevation terrain (especially the Mesopotamian plain around Cizre and Şanlıurfa) makes it one of the hottest regions in Turkey, with recent years showing June peaks of 43–45°C

key_facts: As of June 18, 2026 evening, southeastern Turkey cities are experiencing current temperatures of 25-28°C — within normal range, no active heat wave
Cizre (Şırnak Province) is the hottest of the four target cities, with forecast highs of 39-42°C through late June and a projected 44.3°C on July 2, 2026
Şanlıurfa forecast highs range from 33-40°C through end of June 2026, with a building trend toward late-month peak near 40.7°C on July 2
Diyarbakır forecast highs of 35-41°C, with peak heat expected June 26-28 (39-40°C) and July 2 (41.0°C)
Şırnak city center is significantly cooler due to higher elevation (~1,400m), with forecast highs of 30-36°C
Dust/sandstorm events forecast for June 20 across the region, consistent with hot southerly air advection
Historical June 2024 peak temperatures: Şanlıurfa 43.2°C, Diyarbakır 41.3°C, Cizre 43.3°C
Historical June 2025 peak temperatures: Şanlıurfa 41.3°C, Cizre 44.8°C (June 28), Diyarbakır 40.8°C
June 2024 average daily max for Cizre was 40.0°C; June 2025 was 39.5°C — current 2026 forecast values are broadly consistent
A significant heat intensification is forecast for late June into early July 2026, with multiple cities potentially exceeding 40°C simultaneously
Humidity levels are very low (10-30%), reducing heat index relative to raw temperature but increasing dust/fire risk
